bugs.error_message: EPERM: operation not permitted, mkdir '/Users/nacianilakman/.wolf'
bugs.root_cause: The folder had no local project marker, so OpenWolf selected a parent project root in the user home directory.
bugs.fix: Created a temporary .project marker, ran openwolf init successfully in anil-lib, removed the marker, and rescanned anatomy.

bugs.error_message: zsh: parse error near ')' while running the initial secret-scan regex
bugs.root_cause: The shell command mixed nested single and double quotes inside one regular expression.
bugs.fix: Split the scan into two simpler ripgrep expressions; the rerun completed and found no credentials.

bugs.error_message: git diff --cached --check reports trailing whitespace in imported research Markdown files
bugs.root_cause: The source research documents already contain trailing spaces and blank lines.
bugs.fix: Preserved the imported source text unchanged and treated this as a non-blocking content-format warning for the initial archive commit.

bugs.error_message: remote: Invalid username or token. Password authentication is not supported for Git operations.
bugs.root_cause: The repository used an HTTPS remote before GitHub CLI credentials were configured; no SSH private key was available and the connected GitHub app could not write repository contents.
bugs.fix: Installed GitHub CLI 2.95.0, authenticated the nacianil0 account through GitHub device login, configured HTTPS Git credentials, and pushed main successfully.

bugs.error_message: fatal: could not read Username for 'https://github.com': Device not configured
bugs.root_cause: The GitHub CLI session was valid, but the Git credential helper entry was missing from the active global Git configuration.
bugs.fix: Ran `gh auth setup-git` to register `gh auth git-credential`, then pushed commit 58fe8dd successfully.

bugs.error_message: Significant refactor of
bugs.root_cause: 2 lines replaced/restructured
bugs.fix: Rewrote 7→15 lines (2 removed)

bugs.error_message: ESLint: '.wolf/hooks/*.js' Empty block statement (no-empty); 'last' assigned but never used in reader.spec.ts
bugs.root_cause: The flat ESLint config did not ignore OpenWolf's own .wolf/hooks scripts (which use empty catch blocks), and the e2e spec declared an unused 'last' binding.
bugs.fix: Added '.wolf/**' to the eslint ignores list and removed the unused 'last' variable from the Playwright spec.

bugs.error_message: Playwright: expect(locator('main button[aria-pressed="true"]')).toBeVisible() failed - element(s) not found after clicking the completion toggle
bugs.root_cause: Against `next dev`, the click on the completion button landed before React finished hydrating, so the onClick handler never ran and aria-pressed stayed false.
bugs.fix: Wrapped the toggle in expect(async () => { if (notPressed) await click(); await expect(pressed).toBeVisible(); }).toPass(), so the click retries until hydration completes while only clicking when still unpressed (never double-toggling).

bugs.error_message: Incorrect value in code
bugs.root_cause: Had "Okuma listen hazırlanıyor…"
bugs.fix: Changed to "Kilidi kapat"

bugs.error_message: Missing await
bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value
bugs.fix: Added await to async call

bugs.error_message: Missing await
bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value
bugs.fix: Added await to async call

bugs.error_message: Null/undefined access in
bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Classification batch schema fields existed, but catalog-level Batch 0, gap, and interleaving invariants were not enforced
bugs.root_cause: The initial implementation validated each batch value independently and omitted cross-record ordering plus catalog/frontmatter batch-version comparisons.
bugs.fix: Added ordered catalog invariants for the baseline, contiguous batch numbers, and non-interleaved blocks; also compare classification version and batch against frontmatter during article rendering.

bugs.error_message: The folio header rendered visually as 'Sınıflandırma00' even though its intended text included spacing
bugs.root_cause: Whitespace at the boundary of adjacent flex children collapsed and CSS layout did not create an accessible textual separator.
bugs.fix: Use flex gap for visible separation and set the complete 'Sınıflandırma 00 · N makale' phrase as the heading's accessible label; updated component and E2E assertions to target that contract.

bugs.error_message: Bundled pnpm dependency setup aborted on a non-interactive modules purge and minimum-release-age policy
bugs.root_cause: The runtime selected pnpm 11 while the project pins pnpm 10.22.0; the newer wrapper applied a release-age policy to the already committed frozen lockfile.
bugs.fix: Reinstalled from the unchanged frozen lockfile through `corepack pnpm`, which selected the repository-pinned pnpm 10.22.0, then rebuilt esbuild.

bugs.error_message: `pnpm format:check` reported style differences in nine pre-existing source files
bugs.root_cause: The committed source had not been normalized by the formatter version resolved in the frozen lockfile.
bugs.fix: Ran the repository's pinned Prettier command only on the nine listed files, then reran the complete quality suite.

bugs.error_message: In-app browser read-only evaluation raised TypeError while reading localStorage.getItem during visual verification
bugs.root_cause: The restricted page-evaluation scope exposes DOM inspection primitives but not the window localStorage API.
bugs.fix: Kept persistence verification in the passing Playwright E2E test and limited visual-QC evaluation to DOM state and computed styles.

bugs.error_message: Browser verification recovery command referenced `appliedStyles` after the earlier failed call had not created that binding
bugs.root_cause: The previous evaluation failed before its reusable result binding was initialized, so assigning to that undeclared name in strict mode raised a reference error.
bugs.fix: Created a fresh `appliedStyles2` binding and reran the DOM-only computed-style check successfully.

bugs.error_message: In-app browser expectNavigation timed out even though the login submission had already reached the reader route
bugs.root_cause: The browser helper's URL glob wait did not recognize the completed local navigation pattern, while a fresh DOM snapshot confirmed successful authentication and rendering.
bugs.fix: Used the post-action DOM snapshot as the authoritative navigation signal and continued visual verification from the loaded reader page.

bugs.error_message: The expanded reading-settings panel overflowed beyond the right edge of the desktop viewport
bugs.root_cause: The responsive `sm:right-auto` utility overrode `right-0`, causing the absolutely positioned panel to grow to the right of a toolbar trigger near the viewport edge.
bugs.fix: Removed `sm:right-auto` so the panel remains right-aligned to its trigger, and added an E2E assertion that its bounding box stays within the viewport.

bugs.error_message: TypeScript TS2339: Property 'getByRole' does not exist on type 'HTMLElement' in the new reading-settings component test
bugs.root_cause: The initial test called a Testing Library query method directly on a DOM element instead of creating a scoped query set with `within(element)`.
bugs.fix: Created a scoped dialog query with `within(dialog)` and used it to locate each preference group before querying its buttons.

bugs.error_message: Production login showed 'Erişim yapılandırması eksik. Vercel ortam değişkenlerini kontrol et.' and disabled the password form
bugs.root_cause: The linked Vercel project had no environment variables; production middleware therefore entered its intended fail-closed configuration-error path.
bugs.fix: Added SITE_PASSWORD_SHA256 and AUTH_COOKIE_SECRET as Sensitive variables for Production and Preview, then redeployed the latest production deployment without uploading the dirty local worktree.
